AmazonReviewModeling

Sofia Caceres

2026-04-21

Full Dataset Summary

This report uses the full Amazon review dataset processed with analyze_reviews.sh. The original full data file has one truncated final line, so the analysis uses the complete review records.

total_reviews
## [1] 6427547
ratings_summary %>%
  rename(
    Rating = rating,
    Count = count,
    Percent = percent
  ) %>%
  kable()
Rating Count Percent
1 572868 8.91
2 290055 4.51
3 437555 6.81
4 913751 14.22
5 4213318 65.55 
ggplot(ratings_summary, aes(x = factor(rating), y = count, fill = factor(rating))) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = function(x) format(x, big.mark = ",", scientific = FALSE)) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Review Ratings in the Full Amazon Dataset",
    x = "Rating",
    y = "Count"
  )

Word Counts

For the good/bad comparison, 1-star and 2-star reviews are labeled bad, while 4-star and 5-star reviews are labeled good. Three-star reviews are counted in the rating distribution but excluded from the good/bad word comparison.

top_words <- review_word_counts %>%
  group_by(review_label) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 15) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(top_words, aes(x = reorder_within(word, n, review_label), y = n, fill = review_label)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ review_label, scales = "free") +
  scale_x_reordered() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = function(x) format(x, big.mark = ",", scientific = FALSE)) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Most Common Words in Good vs Bad Amazon Reviews",
    x = "Word",
    y = "Count"
  )

word_counts %>%
  filter(total >= 1000) %>%
  arrange(difference) %>%
  head(20)
## # A tibble: 20 x 7
##    word            bad  good total bad_prop good_prop difference
##    <chr>         <int> <int> <int>    <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl>
##  1 scam           1685   258  1943    0.867     0.133     -0.734
##  2 refund        18073  3089 21162    0.854     0.146     -0.708
##  3 unacceptable   2323   444  2767    0.840     0.160     -0.679
##  4 deceptive      1044   231  1275    0.819     0.181     -0.638
##  5 worthless      6103  1430  7533    0.810     0.190     -0.620
##  6 waste         40450 10087 50537    0.800     0.200     -0.601
##  7 returning     15703  4014 19717    0.796     0.204     -0.593
##  8 worst         14610  3771 18381    0.795     0.205     -0.590
##  9 paperweight     846   225  1071    0.790     0.210     -0.580
## 10 disappointing 10486  2854 13340    0.786     0.214     -0.572
## 11 unusable       5537  1517  7054    0.785     0.215     -0.570
## 12 dissatisfied   1614   446  2060    0.783     0.217     -0.567
## 13 junk          22087  6582 28669    0.770     0.230     -0.541
## 14 expired        3349  1013  4362    0.768     0.232     -0.536
## 15 useless       19803  6010 25813    0.767     0.233     -0.534
## 16 pos            1517   463  1980    0.766     0.234     -0.532
## 17 poorly        10173  3198 13371    0.761     0.239     -0.522
## 18 horrible      18399  5936 24335    0.756     0.244     -0.512
## 19 return        55980 18877 74857    0.748     0.252     -0.496
## 20 returned      26888  9101 35989    0.747     0.253     -0.494
word_counts %>%
  filter(total >= 1000) %>%
  arrange(desc(difference)) %>%
  head(20)
## # A tibble: 20 x 7
##    word          bad   good  total bad_prop good_prop difference
##    <chr>       <int>  <int>  <int>    <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl>
##  1 excelente      14   3887   3901  0.00359     0.996      0.993
##  2 excelent       18   4217   4235  0.00425     0.996      0.991
##  3 linteater       7   1231   1238  0.00565     0.994      0.989
##  4 yum            49   6120   6169  0.00794     0.992      0.984
##  5 addicting      12   1076   1088  0.0110      0.989      0.978
##  6 compliments   116   8591   8707  0.0133      0.987      0.973
##  7 yummy         155  11213  11368  0.0136      0.986      0.973
##  8 lifesaver      28   1854   1882  0.0149      0.985      0.970
##  9 pleasantly    150   9870  10020  0.0150      0.985      0.970
## 10 invaluable     20   1283   1303  0.0153      0.985      0.969
## 11 awsome         46   2866   2912  0.0158      0.984      0.968
## 12 loves        1600  98751 100351  0.0159      0.984      0.968
## 13 prefect        28   1702   1730  0.0162      0.984      0.968
## 14 complements    18   1093   1111  0.0162      0.984      0.968
## 15 godsend        18   1077   1095  0.0164      0.984      0.967
## 16 luv            27   1548   1575  0.0171      0.983      0.966
## 17 unbeatable     40   2148   2188  0.0183      0.982      0.963
## 18 perfect      7294 361664 368958  0.0198      0.980      0.960
## 19 arsenal        26   1261   1287  0.0202      0.980      0.960
## 20 excellent    4090 196948 201038  0.0203      0.980      0.959
bad_words <- word_counts %>%
  filter(total >= 1000) %>%
  arrange(difference) %>%
  head(20)

good_words <- word_counts %>%
  filter(total >= 1000) %>%
  arrange(desc(difference)) %>%
  head(20)

bad_words
## # A tibble: 20 x 7
##    word            bad  good total bad_prop good_prop difference

good_words
## # A tibble: 20 x 7
##    word          bad   good  total bad_prop good_prop difference
##    <chr>       <int>  <int>  <int>    <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl>
##  1 excelente      14   3887   3901  0.00359     0.996      0.993
##  2 excelent       18   4217   4235  0.00425     0.996      0.991
##  3 linteater       7   1231   1238  0.00565     0.994      0.989
##  4 yum            49   6120   6169  0.00794     0.992      0.984
##  5 addicting      12   1076   1088  0.0110      0.989      0.978
##  6 compliments   116   8591   8707  0.0133      0.987      0.973
##  7 yummy         155  11213  11368  0.0136      0.986      0.973
##  8 lifesaver      28   1854   1882  0.0149      0.985      0.970
##  9 pleasantly    150   9870  10020  0.0150      0.985      0.970
## 10 invaluable     20   1283   1303  0.0153      0.985      0.969
## 11 awsome         46   2866   2912  0.0158      0.984      0.968
## 12 loves        1600  98751 100351  0.0159      0.984      0.968
## 13 prefect        28   1702   1730  0.0162      0.984      0.968
## 14 complements    18   1093   1111  0.0162      0.984      0.968
## 15 godsend        18   1077   1095  0.0164      0.984      0.967
## 16 luv            27   1548   1575  0.0171      0.983      0.966
## 17 unbeatable     40   2148   2188  0.0183      0.982      0.963
## 18 perfect      7294 361664 368958  0.0198      0.980      0.960
## 19 arsenal        26   1261   1287  0.0202      0.980      0.960
## 20 excellent    4090 196948 201038  0.0203      0.980      0.959
plot_words <- bind_rows(
  bad_words %>% mutate(group = "More common in bad reviews"),
  good_words %>% mutate(group = "More common in good reviews")
)

ggplot(plot_words, aes(x = reorder(word, difference), y = difference, fill = group)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ group, scales = "free_y") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Words Most Associated with Good vs Bad Reviews",
    x = "Word",
    y = "Good proportion minus bad proportion"
  )

Regression And Random Forest

The logistic regression and random forest models were trained from the terminal with run_full_models.sh. The script streams the full Amazon review file, skips the malformed final line, excludes 3-star reviews from the good/bad classification task, and uses the top 100 full-corpus words as binary document features. Identical feature patterns are compressed with sample weights so the models can use all labeled reviews without writing a huge model matrix to disk.

full_model_summary %>%
  rename(
    Metric = metric,
    Value = value
  ) %>%
  kable()
Metric Value
bad_reviews 862923
complete_records 6427547
good_reviews 5127069
labeled_reviews 5989992
malformed_lines 1
neutral_reviews_skipped 437555
test_reviews 1197491
train_reviews 4792501
unique_test_patterns 655240
unique_train_patterns 2359410 
full_model_metrics %>%
  mutate(
    accuracy = round(accuracy, 4)
  ) %>%
  rename(
    Model = model,
    Accuracy = accuracy,
    `Actual good / predicted good` = actual_good_pred_good,
    `Actual good / predicted bad` = actual_good_pred_bad,
    `Actual bad / predicted good` = actual_bad_pred_good,
    `Actual bad / predicted bad` = actual_bad_pred_bad
  ) %>%
  kable()
Model Accuracy Actual good / predicted good Actual good / predicted bad Actual bad / predicted good Actual bad / predicted bad
logistic_regression 0.8855 998552 26776 110377 61786
random_forest 0.9035 1003014 22314 93223 78940 
model_accuracy <- full_model_metrics %>%
  mutate(
    Model = recode(
      model,
      logistic_regression = "Logistic regression",
      random_forest = "Random forest"
    )
  )

ggplot(model_accuracy, aes(x = Model, y = accuracy, fill = Model)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(round(accuracy * 100, 1), "%")),
    vjust = -0.4
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = function(x) paste0(round(100 * x), "%"),
    limits = c(0, 1)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Model Accuracy on Full Amazon Review Test Set",
    x = "Model",
    y = "Accuracy"
  )

model_confusion <- bind_rows(
  full_model_metrics %>%
    transmute(model, Actual = "Good", Predicted = "Good", Count = actual_good_pred_good),
  full_model_metrics %>%
    transmute(model, Actual = "Good", Predicted = "Bad", Count = actual_good_pred_bad),
  full_model_metrics %>%
    transmute(model, Actual = "Bad", Predicted = "Good", Count = actual_bad_pred_good),
  full_model_metrics %>%
    transmute(model, Actual = "Bad", Predicted = "Bad", Count = actual_bad_pred_bad)
) %>%
  mutate(
    Model = recode(
      model,
      logistic_regression = "Logistic regression",
      random_forest = "Random forest"
    ),
    Actual = factor(Actual, levels = c("Bad", "Good")),
    Predicted = factor(Predicted, levels = c("Good", "Bad"))
  )

ggplot(model_confusion, aes(x = Predicted, y = Actual, fill = Count)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = format(Count, big.mark = ",")), color = "white") +
  facet_wrap(~ Model) +
  coord_equal() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#9ecae1", high = "#08519c") +
  labs(
    title = "Confusion Matrices for Full-Data Models",
    x = "Predicted label",
    y = "Actual label",
    fill = "Count"
  )

logistic_coefficients %>%
  arrange(desc(coefficient_for_bad_review)) %>%
  head(15) %>%
  rename(
    Word = word,
    `Logistic coefficient for bad review` = coefficient_for_bad_review
  ) %>%
  kable()
Word Logistic coefficient for bad review
two 1.4457567
one 1.1781810
doesnt 1.1154168
didnt 0.9039684
dont 0.8683860
after 0.8590177
too 0.8044769
work 0.7555403
even 0.7521095
off 0.7233964
did 0.7162781
back 0.6811983
buy 0.6120776
amazon 0.5676422
then 0.4869110 
logistic_coefficients %>%
  arrange(coefficient_for_bad_review) %>%
  head(15) %>%
  rename(
    Word = word,
    `Logistic coefficient for bad review` = coefficient_for_bad_review
  ) %>%
  kable()
Word Logistic coefficient for bad review
excellent -2.7315989
perfect -2.7272848
love -2.3101346
great -2.0351936
easy -1.8442624
best -1.8097989
nice -1.3378867
works -1.2965909
needed -1.1354993
good -1.0205757
well -0.8970749
little -0.8021446
far -0.7945251
price -0.7423361
need -0.7034922 
logistic_plot_words <- bind_rows(
  logistic_coefficients %>%
    arrange(desc(coefficient_for_bad_review)) %>%
    head(15) %>%
    mutate(group = "More predictive of bad reviews"),
  logistic_coefficients %>%
    arrange(coefficient_for_bad_review) %>%
    head(15) %>%
    mutate(group = "More predictive of good reviews")
)

ggplot(
  logistic_plot_words,
  aes(
    x = reorder_within(word, coefficient_for_bad_review, group),
    y = coefficient_for_bad_review,
    fill = group
  )
) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ group, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_x_reordered() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Most Influential Logistic Regression Words",
    x = "Word",
    y = "Coefficient for bad review"
  )

random_forest_importance %>%
  head(20) %>%
  rename(
    Word = word,
    `Random forest importance` = importance
  ) %>%
  kable()
Word Random forest importance
great 0.1090767
one 0.1029982
two 0.0751973
love 0.0504960
perfect 0.0318304
easy 0.0311889
dont 0.0293142
good 0.0285421
after 0.0278425
best 0.0264939
works 0.0260098
didnt 0.0249415
work 0.0243367
doesnt 0.0236206
did 0.0211522
well 0.0202938
nice 0.0190157
excellent 0.0165678
buy 0.0161870
off 0.0156860 
random_forest_importance %>%
  head(20) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(word, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#238b45") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Top Random Forest Word Importance Scores",
    x = "Word",
    y = "Importance"
  )

Modeling Note

The model results above come from the full terminal run saved in results/full_models. One important modeling choice is that the features are binary indicators for whether each top word appears in a review, rather than exact word counts. This keeps the full-dataset model feasible on the available disk space while still using every labeled complete review through sample weights.
